description: Goal: reduce compiler2\047s quoted-source spine to one coherent model: QuotedExpander owns quoted root rewriting, source publication owns one raw definition/publication transaction, ordinary function bodies expand only behind demand, and compiler2 type/spec/extern fragments travel as preserved token payloads. Signal: item macros and demanded function bodies use one shared quoted expansion engine; source_publish consumes expanded scope fragments instead of owning a second recursive walker; compiler2 no longer reconstructs or re-lexes quoted fragment islands; quote/unquote lowering sits in a narrow, explicit seam; the relevant fz2 source-surface fixtures become tractable without bespoke paths. Strategy: first extract the shared quoted expansion engine and delete the duplicate walkers, then make publication consume expanded scope fragments only, then standardize fragment transport, then narrow quote/unquote lowering, and finally remove leftovers and restate the invariants in docs and telemetry.
title: Compiler2 source spine cleanup: one expander, one publication engine, one fragment transport
